elements.sent-2411-001: There are two weakly coupled regimes of Yang-Mills theory and QCD adiabatically connected to the strongly coupled regime, and furthermore, using semiclassical methods, confinement, chiral symmetry breaking, and multi-branch structure of the vacua can be calculated analytically.
elements.sent-2411-002: One of them is the theory formulated on $\R^3\times S^1$ with suitable deformations where confinement is due to magnetic bions \cite{Unsal:2007jx,Poppitz:2021cxe,Unsal:2008ch and the other one is theory formulated on $\R^2 \times T^2$ with 't Hooft flux (twisted boundary conditions) where confinement is achieved with center vortices \cite{Tanizaki:2022ngt .}}
elements.sent-2411-003: In this work, we focus on the latter and investigate a subtle issue that appears in large-$N$ limit.
elements.sent-2411-004: When Yang-Mills theory formulated on $\R^2 \times T^2$ with twisted boundary conditions and torus compactified on $x^1-x^2$ directions, Polyakov loops on $T^2$ do not commute with each other and they satisfy \cite{Tanizaki:2022ngt \begin{eqnarray}P_1 P_2=P_2 P_1 e^{(2\pi i n_{12}/N)} \label{Polyakov}\end{eqnarray} where $n_{12}\in \Z_N$ and is known as 't Hooft flux.}
elements.sent-2411-005: This algebra can be satisfied and the classical vacuum can be given by clock and shift matrices (see Eq.\eqref{clock ) such that \newline $P_1=S$,$P_2=C$.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Since $tr(P_1^{n_1 P_2^{n_2 )=0$ for any $(n_1,n_2)\neq (0,0)$ mod N, the $\Z_N ^{[0]}\times \Z_N ^{[0]}$ symmetry is unbroken classically.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: For fixed-N, these vacua are stable at sufficiently weak coupling.
elements.sent-2411-008: However, it is not certain whether quantum fluctuations destabilize this classical vacua or not at a fixed-size torus as $N$ is dialed.
elements.sent-2411-009: In fact, there may be non-commutativity of limits between small $L$ and large-$N$.
elements.sent-2411-010: The problem is related to the realization of symmetry breaking and volume independence in the twisted Eguichi Kawai model \cite{GonzalezArroyo:1982hz,GonzalezArroyo:2010ss,Teper:2006sp .}
elements.sent-2411-011: On the other hand, there exists Morita equivalence on $T^2$ such that a U(N) with 't Hooft flux is dual to a noncommutative U(1) gauge theory \cite{Guralnik2,Szabo .}
elements.sent-2411-012: Intriguingly, even a U(1) noncommutative theory is asymptotically free \cite{Barbon , and the same beta function as non-abelian theory appears.}
elements.sent-2411-013: Noncommutative theories also show interesting properties such as UV/IR mixing \cite{Minwalla .}
elements.sent-2411-014: Then, one can ponder about the equivalence of $\Z_N \times \Z_N$ symmetry breaking on the noncommutative theory.
elements.sent-2411-015: It turns out that there exists a tachyonic instability \cite{Bietenholz,ArmoniLopez arising at the one-loop perturbation theory and it leads to spontaneous breaking of translation invariance on the noncommutative side.}
elements.sent-2411-016: It is interesting to see the instability arising from quantum corrections that can dominate the tree-level term.
elements.sent-2411-017: The instability on two torus can be written in terms of fractional momentum modes $\kappa$ and the corresponding electric flux $e_i=p\epsilon_{ij}\kappa _j$ where $p$ depends on noncommutativity parameter through the relation $\theta=\frac{p {N}$ such that \cite{Guralnik \begin{eqnarray}E^2=\frac{1}{R_{nc}^2}(\vec{\kappa}^2-g^2f(\frac{\vec{e}}{N})) \label{instabilitygeneral}\end{eqnarray} where $R_{nc}$ is the radius of noncommutative torus.}}
elements.sent-2411-019: For small values of $\vec{e}$, $f(\frac{\vec{e {N )) \approx \frac{N^2 {\vec{e ^2}$, as we will see directly from \eqref{tachyonic and \eqref{instability .}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Therefore, using 't Hooft coupling $\lambda =g^2 N$, the dispersion relation can be written as $\frac{1 {R_{nc ^2 (\vec{\kappa ^2-\frac{\lambda N {\vec{e ^2 )$.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Indeed, at fixed N, there is no instability at small $\lambda$(small $L$ by asymptotic freedom).
elements.sent-2411-022: But at fixed small $\lambda$(small $L$), an instability would especially emerge for large N.
elements.sent-2411-023: Especially, it is known that the inclusion of adjoint matter fields stabilizes the center symmetry breaking and restores the large-N volume independence in $\R^3\times S^1$ theories \cite{Kovtun-Unsal,Poppitz:2021cxe .}
elements.sent-2411-024: Therefore, it is natural to think that the addition of adjoint fermions would stabilize the symmetry breaking on $T^2 \times \R^2$ as well.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this work, we focus on the latter solution and show that the inclusion of adjoint fermions indeed stabilizes the tachyonic instability in $T^2 \times \R^2$.
elements.sent-2411-026: We will follow the conventions given in \cite{Guralnik .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Noncommutativity is only present on the two torus for the compactification $T^2 \times \R^d$.
elements.sent-2411-028: D is taken as total dimensions whereas d is taken as the number of noncompact and commuting directions.
elements.sent-2411-029: We will have the torus on 1-2 plane and $[x^i,x^j]=2\pi i R^2 \theta \epsilon ^{ij}$ is taken where $\theta$ is dimensionless noncommutativity parameter.
elements.sent-2411-030: The way to achieve this is through the Moyal star product.
elements.sent-2411-031: We will replace all multiplication symbols with star products.
elements.sent-2411-032: We define the Moyal star product of two functions \cite{Guralnik,Szabo,Barbon \begin{eqnarray}f(x)*g(x)=e^{\pi i R^2 \theta \epsilon _{ij} \partial _{x}^i \partial _{y}^j}f(x)g(y)|_{y\rightarrow x} \label{Moyal}\end{eqnarray} On the other hand, Morita duality is an SL(2,Z) duality \cite{Guralnik3,Alvarez .}}
elements.sent-2411-033: If an element of SL(2,Z) is $\begin{pmatrix p q r s \end{pmatrix}$, then ps-qr=1 should be satisfied.}
elements.sent-2411-034: Moreover, one can choose generators of the SL(2,Z) group as $S=\begin{pmatrix 0 -1 1 0 \end{pmatrix}$ ,$T=\begin{pmatrix 1 -1 0 1 \end{pmatrix}$\cite{GapedPhases .}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: According to duality, the noncommutative U(1) theory is dual to the commutative U(N) gauge theory.
elements.sent-2411-036: For rational $\theta=\frac{p {N}$, the conversion table for the variables is as follows \cite{Guralnik2 :.}}
elements.figure-2411-001: \begin{center} \begin{tabular}{ |c|c| } U(1) NC U(N) C $\theta=-\frac{p}{N}$ $\theta=0$ $R^2$ $\frac{R^2}{N^2}$ $\phi =0$ $\phi=-\frac{m}{N}$ \end{tabular} \end{center} \caption{Conversion table for Morita dual theories}
elements.sent-2411-037: These matrices are NxN clock and shift matrices given as follows: \cite{Guralnik \begin{eqnarray}C = \begin{bmatrix} 1 e^{2\pi i \frac{p}{N}} \ddots e^{2\pi i p \frac{(N-1)}{N}} \end{bmatrix} S = \begin{bmatrix} 0 1 0 1 \ddots \ddots 1 \end{bmatrix} \label{clock}\end{eqnarray} and $CS=SCw$ with $w=e^{2\pi i \frac{p {N }$.}}}
elements.formula-2411-004: \begin{equation}C =  1 e^{2\pi i \frac{p}{N}} \ddots e^{2\pi i p \frac{(N-1)}{N}}  S =  0 1 0 1 \ddots \ddots 1  \label{clock}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-038: We will rederive the instability obtained from the gluon polarization diagrams mentioned in \cite{Guralnik .}
elements.sent-2411-039: One can reach Feynman rules for noncommutative U(N) gauge fields from ordinary SU(N) gauge theory by replacing \cite{Barbon,Szabo,Alvarez : \begin{equation}f^{abc} \rightarrow f^{abc} cos(\frac{p_a \times p_b}{2})+d^{abc} sin(\frac{p_a \times p_b}{2}) \label{fromSU(N)}\end{equation} where we defined $p_a \times p_b $ =$p_a \theta^{ab p_b$.}}
elements.sent-2411-040: For U(1) gauge group and $d^{000 =2$ normalization, we will substitute $f^{abc \rightarrow 2 sin(\frac{p_a \times p_b {2 )$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: We have the diagrams given in Figure 1 to compute.\par.

elements.sent-2411-052: We start with introducing the Feynman parameters: \begin{equation}\frac{1}{K^2 (K-P)^2}=\int _0 ^1 \frac{dx}{[(K-P)^2x+K^2(1-x)]^2}=\int _0 ^1 \frac{dx}{[K^2-2KPx+P^2x]^2} \label{Feynmanparameters}\end{equation} In this derivation, as the first important step, we will separate the loop momenta into noncompact and compact directions.
elements.sent-2411-053: We will achieve this by writing $K_\mu \rightarrow K_\mu +L_\mu $ where this time $K_\mu =(k_\alpha,0)$ and $L_\mu =(0,l_i)$, our new $K_\mu$ will be different than the original one.
elements.sent-2411-054: The denominator in \eqref{Feynmanparameters becomes in the new notation \begin{equation}\int _0 ^1 \frac{dx}{[K^2-2KPx+P^2x]^2}=\int _0 ^1 \frac{dx}{[K^2+L^2-2KP_1x-2LP_2x+P^2x]^2} \label{Feynmandenominator}\end{equation} where we also separated the inflowing momenta in noncompact and compact directions.}

elements.sent-2411-081: In this section, similar to the gauge field part, we compute the adjoint fermion-gluon diagram and show that it eliminates the instability.
elements.sent-2411-082: We will include the following lagrangian on top of Yang-Mills \cite{Armoni1 \begin{equation}L_{fermion}=\overline{\psi}i\slashed{\partial}\ast \psi+A_\mu \ast \overline{\psi}\gamma^\mu \ast \psi -\overline{\psi}\ast A_\mu \gamma^\mu \ast \psi\end{equation} with the contribution of the diagram for the massless adjoint fermions will be given for the U(1) gauge group and since the gauge group is U(1), it puts constraints on the matter field representations, only fundamental,anti-fundamental, and adjoint representations are possible \cite{Chaichian .}}

elements.sent-2411-087: In this work, we investigated the $\Z_N \times \Z_N$ symmetry breaking on $T^2 \times \R^2$ compactification of the Yang-Mills theory with twisted boundary conditions.
elements.sent-2411-088: Center symmetry realization exhibits an interesting non-commutativity of limits between small $L$ and large-N: For fixed N, it is stable for sufficiently small $L$, however, at fixed $L$, vacuum is destabilized by the quantum corrections at sufficiently large N.
elements.sent-2411-089: Using Morita equivalence on torus, we converted the problem to the tachyonic instability seen in noncommutative theories.
elements.sent-2411-090: Later, working on the noncommutative side, we discuss ways to overcome the instability.
elements.sent-2411-091: Similar to theories on $\R^3 \times S^1$, the inclusion of adjoint fermions work changes quantum fluctuations and it favors stabilization of the center symmetry.
elements.sent-2411-092: One is to understand what would center vortices correspond to on the noncommutative side.
elements.sent-2411-093: For a complete understanding of confinement on the noncommutative side, this is a necessary step to know.
elements.sent-2411-094: Moreover, it is curious to study Seiberg-Witten on the same setup.
elements.sent-2411-095: Especially, on $T^2 \times T^2$ compactification with 't Hooft flux turned on at each $T^2$ fractional topological charge object can be observed.
elements.sent-2411-096: It is also interesting to use Poisson duality, to show how center vortex theory continuously connects to the Seiberg Witten theory as already conducted for theories on $\R^3 \times S^1$ \cite{Poppitz:2011wy and noncommutative analogies for these.}
elements.sent-2411-097: In particular, it would be interesting to understand the structure of center-vortices in ${\cal N =2$ Seiberg-Witten theory and its small mass deformation to ${\cal N =1$.}}
elements.sent-2411-098: Since the former is non-confining on $\mathbb R^4$, and the latter is confining, we expect an interesting change in the structure of the center-vortices on $\mathbb R^2 \times T^2$ with $n_{12}$ units of 't Hooft flux.
elements.sent-2411-099: This investigation can be performed by combining ideas along the lines of \cite{Hayashi:2024psa, Hayashi:2024yjc, Guvendik:2024umd, Poppitz:2011wy .. \acknowledgments C.G.}
